title: A special issue of Essays in Biochemistry on evolutionary developmental biology.
author_string: McGregor, Alistair P, Buffry, Alexandra D, Vroomans, Renske M A
abstract: Evolutionary developmental biology (or evo devo) is a broad field that aims to understand how developmental processes evolve and how this underpins phenotypic change and organismal diversification. This encompasses a need to understand theoretical concepts in evolutionary biology and how tissues, cells, genes, proteins and regulatory elements function and evolve. The articles in this special issue review key topics in the field of evo devo including advances in theory and methodology as well as our latest knowledge about molecular, cellular and organismal functionality and diversification.
